When creating a Sympathetic Drive, you must use a Magmarizer, Electrizer, Metal Coat, Up-Grade, or Dubious Disc. A Pokébot may have multiple Sympathetic Drives installed, but no two that bestow the same effect. When installed, the Sympathetic Drive has an effect based on the item used to make it:
»» Magmarizer - the Pokébot learns Fire Punch, Flamethrower, and Fire Spin OR the Pokébot gains Heatproof and is immune to being Frozen.
»» Electrizer - the Pokébot learns Thunder Punch, Discharge, and Magnet Rise OR the Pokébot gains Motor Drive and is immune to Paralysis.
»» Metal Coat - the Pokébot learns Iron Head, Flash Cannon, and Iron Defense OR the Pokébot is now Steel Type and ignores its base Pokébot type relations.
»» Up-Grade - the Pokébot learns Tri-Attack, Conversion and Thunder Wave OR the Pokébot gains Download and Analytic.
»» Dubious Disc - the Pokébot learns Metronome, Acupressure, and Disable OR the Pokébot gains Moody and may be healed by Restoratives, Refreshments, and Moves.

The Pokébot gains the Regenerator Ability. Additionally, once per day, as an Extended Action it may gain a Digestion/Food Buff as if using Leftovers.

When installed, choose up to two of the Pokébot’s Moves and one of its Abilities. Whenever a Pokemon with Wired is controlling this Pokébot, it adds the chosen Moves to its Movelist and gains the chosen Ability. While being controlled, a Pokébot loses access to its other Moves. Rotom inhabiting this Pokébot are treated as if in Appliance Form.
Increase the maximum complexity of the Pokébot by 2.

The Pokébot may directly interface with computers, accessing the data through an Eye Slot Augmentation, external visor, Pokédex, or internal computer. The Pokébot can communicate wirelessly with others over networks using the Datajack.
(Eyes) The Pokébot can see in the IR spectrum and gains the Darkvision Capability.

(Eyes) The Pokébot cannot have their Accuracy lowered, and their attacks cannot have Accuracy Penalties.

(Limbs) The Pokébot gains a +2 bonus to their Power Capability and +1 to both High and Long Jump Capabilities.
(Limbs) The Pokébot gains a +2 bonus to their Power Capability and +1 to both High and Long Jump Capabilities.  The Pokébot recovers from Injuries twice as fast and heals one more Injury per day.

(Limbs) Choose Small Melee or Short Range. The Pokébot is always treated as equipped with the chosen type of Weapon at a Simple Quality, without consuming an equipment slot, and it may not be disarmed. When installed, choose Bash!, Cheap Shot, or Pierce! for this Weapon’s Adept Move. The Pokébot may use Technology Education to qualify for Moves from this Weapon.

(Limbs) Choose Small Melee or Short Range. The Pokébot is always treated as equipped with the chosen type of Weapon at a Fine Quality, without consuming an equipment slot, and it may not be disarmed. When installed, choose Bash!, Cheap Shot, or Pierce! for this Weapon’s Adept Move, with Deadly Strike as this weapon's Master Move. The Pokébot may use Technology Education to qualify for Moves from this Weapon.

(Body) Three times a day as an Extended Action, the Pokébot may gain a Digestion Buff as if they had consumed Leftovers. This does not take up one of the Pokébot’s normal Digestion Buff slots, but they may only have one Digestion/Food Buff gained this way at a time.

Once a Scene, the user may cure themselves of all Persistent Status Afflictions as a Standard Action.

The Pokébot gains a +2 boost to their maximum Complexity and counts their used Augmentations as 2 lower for the purposes of Augmentation Shock. The Pokébot can have multiple Augmentations in the same Slot.
